title: Cynanchum antsiranense
taxonomy: Cynanchum antsiranense is classified within the family Apocynaceae. This species belongs to the genus Cynanchum, which consists of approximately 350 species of twining or climbing plants. The Apocynaceae family includes various plants known as dogbanes, oleander, or swallowworts, many of which are toxic. Within the family Apocynaceae, the genus Cynanchum is part of the tribe Asclepiadeae, which also includes the genus Asclepias. The classification of Cynanchum antsiranense reflects its botanical relationships with other species in the Apocynaceae family.

intro: Cynanchum antsiranense, also known as Antsiaranan milkvine, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its twining habit and milky latex. Traditionally used in Malagasy medicine, Cynanchum antsiranense is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cynanchum antsiranense is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and also used in the form of a tincture or extract, to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
